Life is a game of chance, not of luck Is it really so terrible to want something in life? To have the knowledge that everything is going to be okay. I don't. Life doesn't teach us this, life teaches us struggle almost every day to either physically, mentally or emotionally get through each and every day until we run out. Let's be clear, I'm not talking about money, I'm not talking about job security, I'm not even talking about making sure you're safe. I'm talking about you. You ability to see things how they are and to stop overseeing how things might be. We spend every day thinking to ourselves how it would be better if our lives had turned out a different way, with different people in it and with different days. This is not the way things should be. Life is a test, and a cruel one at that. Whoever tells you that we fall so we can learn to get ourselves up clearly hasn't fallen hard enough. There comes a point where the fight to get back up is a lot harder than the fall itself, so we learn not to climb too high to avoid the risk all together. Say what you want but this is the general way of the world, the "safe" option, when in reality for some people this isn't safe at all. It's survival. The happiest man alive could have everything he ever wanted, could have done everything he ever wanted to do and could be loved and respected by everyone around him. This man would still no pain, sorrow, betrayal and rage, so wtf is the point?

Started a new job today I’ve been battling (mild) anxiety for a few years now, occasionally get panic attacks from stressful situations/the unknown. Mostly stemming from esteem issues. I started a new job today and at the end of it had a full blown panic Attack because I felt like an imposter and incompetent to deal with this new environment. I had to start my first day at home due to the covid-19 outbreak and all support and communications were remote. Everyone I met online told me things move really fast here and learning curve is steep and I got very overwhelmed and started crying, and haven’t stopped for an hour now. How do I stop feeling like this?

I'm so ashamed of being depressed I'm always convinced that even acknowledging my issues or things that have happened to me in the past is somehow "playing the victim" and I need to be ashamed of it and never bring any of it up. Whenever I do mention any of it I'm so nervous and it makes me extremely uncomfortable. Usually if I bring it up I'm drunk. I guess I always just want someone to tell me that I'm allowed to feel sad, and that I've gone through some shitty stuff. I don't know why I want that validation, and it makes me disgusted with myself that I'm really that person who seeks attention like that. I tried to kill myself in October, and I only told one person and they asked me if I did it for attention from them. Its just made me so ashamed, and disgusted at myself. I can't tell anyone anything, or I'm terrified that they think I want them to see me as a victim. I'm so alone. I guess I'm finally learning that this is truly my burden to bear and mine alone.

Without a stable living situation and losing hope Just over a week ago I left a really shitty situation with parents who have treated me pretty awfully for years and I am finally free - however, I'm homeless. I have been staying with friends and I spend some time in crisis accommodation provided by my psychologist after I finally opened up to her about everything they have been doing to me, and it's just been really hard. I'm having trouble getting the legal documents I need to secure a stable living situation, in my country so much of that is reliant on parental confirmation even though I am a legal adult, and I met up with my mum today to try and talk things out with her but I got brutalised and put down and just made to feel really worthless all over again. She couldn't even not be cruel to me after meeting for just one day after not seeing eachother for awhile, so I have decided to go no contact. I feel really helpless. I feel like the systems in place quite literally fuck you over if you have abusive families, needing their birth certificates to verify things. I feel like it has been made near impossible for me to live independently without them but I know I can never go back because they have put me through so much trauma and so many things I know to not be okay and they have no remorse, and I'm never going to put up with that ever again. I really don't want to go on anymore, I feel so weak, I have been trying so hard to get everything in order for 10 days straight and it has amounted to nothing, and I really feel like I have no other choice,

Sometimes I just don’t want to be here As the title says, sometimes I just don’t want to do this anymore. I’m not in any particularly bad situations in life (which makes me feel bad for feeling this way to start with). Everything will be good for a while and I’ll be good, just living my life, and occasionally I just crash. I’ll be really sad (on the inside, while I go about my way on the outside) and while a lot of times music, or staying busy helps, other times I just get so sad and anxious that I just don’t want to really be here feeling like this. I’ve never been to a doctor or therapist about anything so nothing is diagnosed. But it worries me when when I start thinking about suicide, and makes me think I should seek help.
